disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden two-hour behavioural crisis — inconsolable screaming, throwing objects and repeated head-banging — in a non-verbal, profoundly deaf 4-year-old, beginning after his communication tablet broke
trigger_question_examples: What has been happening and when did it start?
Is this different from his usual frustration?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He has been screaming, throwing things and banging his head on the floor for about two hours. It started after his tablet broke and he couldn't tell us what he wanted. We've never seen him like this for this long.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Localising behaviour — the child is repeatedly pulling and grabbing at his left ear
trigger_question_examples: Is he holding, pulling or touching any part of his body?
Have you noticed him grabbing at his ears, mouth or tummy?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you ask — yes, he keeps pulling hard at his left ear over and over.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: He feels hot and sweaty; a home thermometer read about 38.2 degrees
trigger_question_examples: Does he feel hot? Have you been able to take his temperature?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He feels really hot and is sweating a lot. We managed a home thermometer and it said about 38.2.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Repeated head-banging against hard floor tiles has already caused a forehead graze and a small bump
trigger_question_examples: Has he hurt himself while doing this?
Can you see any marks or swelling where he has hit his head?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — he's grazed his forehead and there's a small bump coming up from banging it on the tiles.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: He is completely non-verbal and profoundly deaf and cannot describe or localise pain himself
trigger_question_examples: Can he tell you what is wrong or where it hurts?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He can't tell us anything — he's deaf and doesn't speak. He normally uses picture apps on his tablet, but that's broken.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: He has taken nothing by mouth and no pain relief has been given because he cannot be calmed enough to swallow
trigger_question_examples: Has he had any pain relief today?
Is he able to take anything by mouth?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing — he won't take anything by mouth and we can't get near enough to give him anything.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No vomiting, no drowsiness or difficulty rousing, and he is still moving all limbs normally
trigger_question_examples: Has he vomited, gone unusually sleepy or become hard to wake?
Is he moving both arms and legs normally?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No vomiting and he's not sleepy — if anything he's the opposite. He's moving everything normally.
